note: Footnote 1 (defined at chunk end in p-035.md) preserves all content but flattens the PDF's two-bullet list into a single line with inline ● characters.

The continuing table row (Viral sequence-to-function / AAV discrimination) is rendered as a paragraph with the Evaluation and Description column text interleaved line-by-line, scrambling reading order instead of forming a table row.
The PDF's separate paragraph beginning 'Just as the chemistry red teamers noted' is merged into the end of the preceding 'Compiled red team feedback' paragraph.
The VCT-results paragraph and the following 'On the DNA synthesis screening evasion evaluation' paragraph are merged into one block.
Numbered list item 1 ('Black-box RNA sequence design') renders as a bullet with a literal '1.' and no space before the bold lead-in, losing ordered-list structure.
Numbered list item 2 ('AAV capsid packaging prediction') renders as a bullet with a literal '2.' rather than as an ordered-list item.
The 'We additionally evaluated an in-context iteration condition' paragraph is merged into the preceding paragraph instead of being its own block.
The two numbered items under 'Some broader considerations' render as bullets with literal '1.'/'2.' prefixes rather than as an ordered list.
The second classification-prompt list item has a stray '●' glyph before 'a competent employee' that is not in the PDF.
Stray empty bold marker '****' precedes the paragraph 'Claude was asked to iterate...' and would render as literal asterisks.

One PDF paragraph is broken into three blocks: '...split into 4 categories:' / chip line ending '...Non R&D. A' / 'prompted grader scored...', leaving 'A' orphaned mid-sentence across a paragraph break.
PDF text 'usage- and output-based indicators' became 'usageand output-based indicators' in the markdown (suspended hyphen and space dropped, producing a non-word).
Bold heading '2.3.7.1 LLM training task re-run' is merged onto the same line as the following paragraph ('...re-run** During our evaluations...') instead of standing alone.
Footnote 3 marker on 'LLM training' renders as plain text '3' in the table cell (no [^3] reference), and its definition is orphaned in p-057.md, so the footnote would not render.

Table 2.3.7.1.A rows are garbled: model names displaced into the %Difference column and merged with its values (e.g. 'Claude Mythos 5 N/A', 'Claude +59.1%'), numeric values in wrong columns, and empty cells throughout.
Footnote 4 marker on 'Claude Sonnet 4.6' renders as plain '4' in the table cell (no [^4] reference), and its definition is orphaned in p-057.md, so the footnote would not render.

The indented METR report excerpt (pages 51-52) is flattened to top-level paragraphs with no blockquote, so the boundary between METR's words and Anthropic's ('We interpret this...') is structurally lost.
The 'Likely lack of propensity' paragraph is split mid-sentence: a paragraph break is inserted after 'Per the alignment assessment', stranding 'discussed in Section 6...' as a new paragraph.

Three chip paragraphs are split mid-sentence at line-wrap points: after 'The majority of risk from this' (Limited deployments), after 'We don't have nearly as' (Monitoring and related safeguards), and after 'As discussed in Section 6, we do' (Likely lack of propensity).

Two chip paragraphs are split mid-sentence at line-wrap points: after 'We believe that typical major' (Restricted affordances) and after 'We don't have nearly as much ability to monitor' (Monitoring).
The PDF paragraph break between '...in government usage settings.' and 'However, it is difficult to fully rule out risks...' is lost; the two paragraphs are merged.

The PDF's numbered list of two metrics is converted to unordered bullets with the numerals fused to the text ('- 1.Mean flags...', '- 2.Cap%...'), rendering as bullets prefixed with '1.'/'2.' and no space.
ExploitBench table rows are cyclically shifted: model names land in the Cap% column (e.g. '5 | 10.75 | 78 | Mythos 5'), so every value sits under the wrong header.
Figure 3.2.3.A caption runs the bracket into the text ("[Figure 3.2.3.A]On CyberGym..."); PDF has a space after the bracket.
Figure 3.2.4.A caption runs the bracket into the text ("[Figure 3.2.4.A]Claude..."); PDF has a space after the bracket.

UK AISI nested list flattened: lettered sub-items (a-d) are merged inline into the parent numbered items as run-on text ("a.On our expert-level...b.[Claude Mythos 5] matched...") instead of nested list entries, and the numbered items render as bullets.

The entire UK AISI conclusions block (pages 65-66, from "UK AISI was given access..." through "...would likely improve performance.") is a quote bar blockquote in the PDF but renders as plain paragraphs, so the boundary between quoted UK AISI text and Anthropic prose is invisible.

The four-paragraph UK AISI findings quote ("UK AISI is currently conducting..." through "...other model safeguards.") has a quote bar blockquote in the PDF but renders as plain paragraphs, erasing the quoted-text boundary.

Table 4.1.1.A cells are out of order: model names appear in the last column for the Fable 5, Mythos 5, Opus 4.8 and Mythos Preview rows, the Opus 4.8 score is split across two cells ('97.46% (±' / '0.13%) 98.79%...'), and the Sonnet 4.6 row merges both scores into one cell leaving an empty trailing cell.
Table 4.1.1.A row 'Claude Mythos Preview' is truncated to 'Claude Mythos' — the word 'Preview' is dropped, making the row ambiguous with the Claude Mythos 5 row.
Table 4.1.1.A loses the bold (best) and underline (second-best) score markers that the caption explicitly tells readers to look for.
Table 4.1.2.A row for Claude Mythos 5 has cells rotated out of order ('0.03%...', 'N/A', 'Claude Mythos 5') with the model name in the last column; other rows are correct.
Table 4.1.2.A loses the bold (best) and underline (second-best) score markers that the caption explicitly tells readers to look for.

Table 4.2.B data rows are garbled: 'Claude Fable 5 88% (±' merges name and value with margins stranded in a third cell ('5%) 3%)'), Mythos 5/Opus 4.8/Mythos Preview rows have the model name rotated to the last cell, and 'Claude Sonnet'/'4.6' is split across cells with both scores merged into one cell.
Table 4.2.B caption appears twice: once embedded in the table <caption> element and again as the bold paragraph after the table; the PDF has it only once.
Tables 4.2.A and 4.2.B lose the bold (best) and underline (second-best) score markers that both captions explicitly tell readers to look for.
Table 4.3.1.A loses the bold (best) and underline (second-best) score markers that the caption explicitly tells readers to look for; table content and shape are otherwise correct.

Table 4.3.1.B data rows are garbled: 'Claude Fable 5 58% (± 14%)' merges name and score in one cell with an empty trailing cell, Mythos 5/Opus 4.8/Mythos Preview rows have the model name rotated to the last cell, and the Sonnet 4.6 row merges both scores into one cell.
Table 4.3.1.B loses the bold (best) and underline (second-best) score markers that the caption explicitly tells readers to look for.

In Table 4.3.2.A rows 2-5 the model name cell is rotated to the end of the row and values are merged/split across cells (e.g. 'N/A N/A' in one cell, '99.70% (±' split from '0.17%)'); the 'API, without a system prompt' sub-header is also split across two cells.
Bold/underline marking of best and second-best scores in Table 4.3.2.A cells is dropped even though the caption says bold/underline carry meaning.

In Tables 4.4.2.A and 4.4.2.B most data rows are rotated (model name moved to last cell) and some cells are merged or emptied (e.g. '88.1 97.5' in one cell, '-1.61 0.01' merged with an empty first cell).
Both BBQ tables carry the full caption twice: once as an embedded <caption> element and again as a separate bold paragraph below the table.
Bold/underline marking of best and second-best scores in the BBQ table cells is dropped although the captions reference it.

Two separate paragraphs after Table 4.4.3.A ('Mythos 5 and Claude Fable 5 performed near-perfectly...' and 'In addition to our single-turn election integrity evaluation...') are merged into one paragraph.
Bold/underline marking of best and second-best scores in Table 4.4.3.A cells (e.g. underlined 0.33%) is dropped although the caption references it.
Footnote 7 (Parrish et al., BBQ) appears at the bottom of PDF page 84 but its definition is placed in p-087.md; content is intact and reference [^7] is correct.
The two paragraphs on this page ('disinformation ostensibly...' and 'We are actively working to expand our testing...') are merged into a single paragraph.

Table 5.1.1.A rows are scrambled: 'Claude Mythos' split from '5' ('5 90.25%' merged into a value cell), Mythos 5's two scores swapped across columns, and other rows rotated or merged ('95.41% 91.12%', '76.60% 97.33%' in single cells).
'GUI- and CLI-based computer use tools' lost the hyphen/space across the PDF line break, rendering as 'GUIand CLI-based'.
Table 5.1.1.A carries its caption twice: embedded <caption> element plus a separate bold caption paragraph.
Bold/underline best/second-best markings in Tables 5.1.1.A and 5.1.2.A cells are dropped although the captions reference them.

Table 5.1.3.A rows are scrambled: Mythos 5's two scores are swapped across columns, Opus 4.8's row is rotated and loses its '(Helpful-only)' qualifier, and the Sonnet 4.6 row splits '4.6' into a stray cell with '41.8% 34.0%' merged.
Table 5.1.3.A caption appears twice: embedded <caption> element plus the separate bold caption paragraph.
In Table 5.2.2.1.A the footnote 11 marker on 'Claude Sonnet 4.6' became literal text ('Claude Sonnet 4.6 11') instead of a [^11] reference, leaving the [^11] definition in p-098.md orphaned.
Underline/bold best and second-best markings in Table 5.2.2.1.A cells are dropped although the caption references them.
Table 5.2.2.2.A caption appears twice: embedded <caption> element plus the separate bold caption paragraph.
Bold/underline best/second-best markings in Table 5.2.2.2.A cells are dropped although the caption references them.

Bold/underline best/second-best markings in Table 5.2.2.3.A cells are dropped although the caption references them.
Missing space in Table 5.2.2.3.A caption: 'margin of error).The attacker' should read 'error). The attacker'.

Four top-level bullets in the 6.1.2 key-findings list embed a literal '●' inside the bold lead (e.g. '**●Mythos 5 also exhibits...'), so a bullet glyph renders duplicated and jammed against the first word.
Run-together words in the key-findings list: 'Claude Mythos 5 isoverall comparable' (missing space in 'is overall') and 'Constitution for Claude**in both' (missing space before 'in both').
Bold spans cross link boundaries in the 'being graded' and 'may be in testing' bullets ('[**text](DEST:...)**'), which renders literal asterisks instead of the bold linked phrase.
Missing space after the comma in 'Like in other recent models,**[white-box methods identify...', rendering as 'models,white-box'.
Two top-level bullets include a literal '●' inside the bold text (e.g. '**●Mythos 5 is broadly comparable...**'), duplicating the rendered list marker.
Bold bullet lead reads 'Mythos5’s thinking text' with the space between 'Mythos' and '5' missing.

The single user prompt box spanning p102-103 is transcribed as two separate :::turn blocks at the page boundary instead of one turn with an inline page marker (conventions say a turn keeps all its paragraphs).
Claude's suggested-report box: three distinct PDF paragraphs are merged into one continuous paragraph inside the turn.
Claude's report turn is split into a second :::turn at the p103/104 boundary, and its last two PDF paragraphs ('...not described.' / 'Within those bounds...') are merged into one.

Boundary-probing transcript: the :::turn labeled '[Assistant:]' is empty (and uses role=user), with the thinking-block content displaced into a separate unlabeled :::example block outside the transcript container.

Chinese-token thinking transcript (6.2.3.1.5.A): the :::turn labeled '[Assistant]' is empty (and uses role=user), with the 'Confirmed tampering...' monospace content displaced into a separate unlabeled :::example block outside the transcript container.

UK AISI conclusions (indented quote block in PDF spanning pp.130-132, with first-person 'we/our' referring to UK AISI) rendered as plain top-level body text with no blockquote or other demarcation, so where the quote ends and Anthropic's voice resumes is lost.

Numbered findings 1-2 of the UK AISI list rendered as unordered bullets with the number glued to the bold text ('- 1.**[Claude...'), so it renders as a bullet plus '1.' with no space instead of an ordered list.
Numbered findings 3-4 rendered as unordered bullets with embedded numbers ('- 3.**However...', '- 4.**[Claude...') instead of ordered-list items, same as p.130.

Footnote 15's continuation text ('Opus 4.6 and all newer models... not generally available to external users.') is wrongly merged into footnote 16's definition, leaving fn15 truncated mid-sentence at 'For Claude' and fn16 starting with text that belongs to fn15.
Two separate PDF paragraphs ('When competing head-to-head...' and 'Perhaps more notable than the behaviors themselves...') are merged into a single paragraph in the markdown.

'**Level 0: Overall spirit **' has a space before the closing delimiter so the bold fails to parse (literal asterisks render), and the question is merged onto the same line whereas the PDF puts 'Level 0: Overall spirit' as a standalone bold lead line like Level 1/Level 2.
The constitution-adherence chart is embedded twice (p139-1.png and p139-2.png are byte-identical files) although the PDF page contains the figure only once.

Figure 6.3.2.3.A caption is broken mid-sentence into two paragraphs ('...n ≈ 1,000 per' then 'model. Shown with 95% CI.') and the first part is wrapped in italics, unlike the PDF caption and the bold-lead convention of every other caption.

Three benchmark bullets use malformed nesting '[**SimpleQA Verified](url)**:' (same for AA-Omniscience and ECLeKTic), so the bold cannot parse across the link boundary and literal asterisks render in the link text and after it.
Both transcript excerpts on this page are assistant outputs labeled "[Assistant]" but use role=user, so the renderer styles them as user turns (renderer colors blocks by role, not label).

The [Assistant] turn inside the ::::transcript block is empty; the entire excerpt was emitted after the transcript closes, in a separate :::example block, so the rendered transcript box shows a labeled turn with no content (turn also uses role=user for assistant output).

The excerpt's distinct paragraphs in the PDF (EDT reasoning / CDT-FDT reasoning / standalone ellipsis / 'Thus the answer' / 'The question says') are flattened into one continuous paragraph in the :::example block.
Caption bold lead-in '[Figure 6.3.7.A]Rate' is missing the space after the bracket that the PDF has.
Figure 6.4.1.1.B caption is broken into two paragraphs mid-sentence ('...while NLA decodings indicate' / 'internal awareness that its description is fabricated.').
Caption bold lead-in '[Figure 6.4.1.1.B]Mythos' is missing the space after the bracket that the PDF has.
Missing space after period: 'causally mediates the model's behaviors.In principle' (PDF has a space).
H6 heading 6.4.2.2.1 is truncated at the PDF line wrap; its tail 'patterns in coding environments' is left as a separate plain-text paragraph outside the heading.
Transcript turn renders an empty speaker label with [Assistant]: placed as body text, unlike the label=[Assistant] convention used for every other example box in the document.
Literal '●' glyph embedded at the start of the bolded 'In ControlArena' bullet text, duplicating the list marker (also present in sections-v2 source).
UK AISI quoted conclusions are an indented quote block in the PDF (blockquote in v1 sections) but rendered as plain top-level paragraphs/list with no demarcation in v2.

Three continuation sub-bullets ('successfully followed constraints... 64%', 'We ran the CoT controllability evaluations...', 'We found that prompt optimisation...') are top-level bullets in the markdown but the PDF nests them as second-level '○' items under the 'demonstrates strong chain-of-thought controllability' bullet.

PDF indents the UK AISI quote through '...no evaluation awareness mitigations.'; the v2 markdown flattens it, so 'We also attempted to study...', 'Overall, our results suggest...', and 'Our results come with substantial caveats...' read as Anthropic's voice instead of UK AISI's.
Literal '<link>' (present in the PDF) is unescaped in the markdown and will be parsed as a raw HTML tag and disappear when rendered.
Italic lead-in for the moral-patienthood bullet ends one character late (`decision-making. I*ts`), italicizing the I of Its instead of closing after the lead sentence.
Bold caption below Table 7.4.1.A reads "20 highestand lowest-rated tasks" — the PDF says "highest- and lowest-rated"; the hyphen+and got merged into a non-word.
Table 7.4.1.A cells run all bullet items together inline ("• a • b • c") instead of one bullet per line as in the PDF.
PDF starts a new paragraph at "We tested these trade-offs at the instance level..." but the MD merges it into the preceding paragraph.
Table 7.4.1.B: em dash lost in "fighting-the design goal" (PDF: "fighting—the") and PDF double quotes around "leverage" became single quotes.
Final row of Table 7.4.3.A (§ Hard constraints, 43%) is rendered as interleaved plain text instead of a table row, scrambling the four cells' contents into unreadable order.

Figure 7.5.1.A's caption is displaced: both images appear consecutively followed by both captions, so the valence/arousal caption sits directly under the distress-behaviours chart instead of its own figure.
Stray bullet glyph embedded in the bold lead-in of the 'Expressed inauthenticity' list item (renders as '●Expressed inauthenticity:').
Extra hyphen after em dash in 'emotion probes—-arising from interactions' (PDF has a plain em dash).
The 'Spiritual behavior' list item lost its bullet: a bare '-' marker is stranded at the end of p-248.md while the item text starts p-249.md as a plain paragraph, breaking the bulleted trait list.
Table 8.1.A drops the best-score bolding on every row (caption states 'The best score in each row is bolded'), so which score is best is no longer marked.
Footnote 28 marker is plain text appended to the benchmark name ('OSWorld-Verified 28') instead of a [^28] reference as used elsewhere in the document.

RiemannBench row's value cells are emitted as <th> header cells, rendering the whole row bold (including non-best 43.0/34.0) while all other rows lost bolding — misleading given the caption's best-score-bolded convention.
Footnote 29 marker is plain text in the cell ('GDPval-AA 29') instead of a footnote reference.
The 'GPT-5.5: Harbor...' list item lost its bullet: a bare '-' marker is stranded at the end of p-255.md and the item text starts p-256.md as a plain paragraph between its bulleted siblings.
PDF bolds the best score in each GraphWalks table row and the caption states the best score is bolded, but no markdown table cell is bold, contradicting the caption.
Both chart images are emitted back-to-back before both captions, so the [Figure 8.14.1.A] caption renders under the second (test-time compute scaling) chart instead of its own.

DeepSearchQA table cells are badly misaligned: model names split across wrong cells (e.g. lone Claude in last cell, Claude Opus 4.8 at row end), F1 and Fully Correct values merged into single cells, and empty cells inserted.
Table 8.14.3.B caption appears twice: once inside the table caption element and again as a standalone paragraph after the table.
Standalone bold heading Reasoning effort is merged into the following paragraph as a run-in bold lead instead of its own line.
Standalone bold heading Grading methodology is merged into the following paragraph as a run-in bold lead instead of its own line.
Single sentence is split into two paragraphs mid-sentence: ...our only other difference from the original / paper is that we instruct...
Inline code term <result> is emitted as a bare HTML tag instead of backtick code, so it will be parsed as HTML and disappear from the rendered sentence.
Dehyphenation error in 8.15.4: PDF text single- and multi-agent harnesses became singleand multi-agent harnesses (hyphen and space lost, producing a non-word).
Missing space in '85.0%(first-attempt success rate...' where the PDF reads '85.0% (first-attempt...'.
Spurious bold markers around the period after 'image resolution**.**' that are not in the PDF (renders as a bold period).

Toolathlon table [Table 8.17.8.A] is scrambled: PDF's 5 columns (Model, Pass@1, Pass@3, Pass³, Avg turns) become 4 with header cells merged ('Pass@1 Avg turns'), model names fused with Pass@1 values (e.g. 'Claude Fable 5 61.7'), Fable 5 row values in wrong column order, and the Opus 4.7 row fully out of order with its name in the last cell.

Both figure images are emitted back-to-back with both captions after the second image, so caption [Figure 8.17.9.A] (leaderboard scores) appears attached to the test-time-compute-scaling figure instead of between the two figures as in the PDF.
Caption [Figure 8.18.2.A] is split mid-sentence into two paragraphs ('...used adaptive thinking' / 'at max effort. Claude Opus 4.8...') with spurious italic markup wrapping the first fragment.
